title: Bead Beating in Custom Buffer Followed by XP Bead Cleanup (NGS Workflow)

steps: %s %s ComponentAmount to addNaCl (5M)2 mLTris-HCl pH 8.3 (1M)1 mLEDTA pH 9.0 (0.5M)0.2 mLTriton X-1001 mLH2O95.8 mL %s %s ComponentAmount for 100 mLTris-HCl 1M pH 81 mLEDTA 0.5M20 uL
Transfer the inactivated bacterial suspension to a new well-labeled Starsted screw cap tube containing ~ 250 µL of Mini-BeadBeater Zirconia-Silicate Beads, -0.1 mm
Bead beat the lysate at 6.5m/s for 00:00:45 with 00:02:00 rest on ice between runs
Add 250 µL (1.2X volume) AMPure XP beads and mix by pipetting up and down ten times
Centrifuge at max speed 10000 rcf, Room temperature, 2 minutes and transfer the supernatant to a new well-labeled tube. Take care not to transfer beads or cell debris.
Place on magnetic rack and wait for the solution to clear, ~ 00:02:00
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00
Discard the supernatant and wash the beads twice with freshly prepared 70 % (v/v) ethanol without disrupting the beads
Dry the beads briefly, ~ 00:02:00 . Tip: Remove residual EtOH with a p10 pipette
Immediately after the bead pellet becomes opaque, remove the tube from magnetic rack and resuspend in 20 µL of Low EDTA Tris Buffer . Ensure all beads are in solution.
Incubate at room temperature for 00:05:00
Place on magnetic rack, wait for the solution to become clear ~ 00:02:00 , and transfer the eluted DNA to a new well-labeled tube
If working with MGIT tubes, inactivate for 01:00:00 at 80 °C according to the South African National Health Laboratory Services (NHLS) facility standard operating procedures.

If required (i.e., to remove samples for processing outside a BSL-3), decontaminate MGIT culture in the original tube and media for 01:00:00 at 80 °C according to the standard operating procedures of the South African National Health Laboratory Services (NHLS) facility or your facilities guidelines.
rpm, °C, Centrifuge at max speed for 00:10:00
Resuspend in 350 µL of Custom Triton Buffer
Repeat for a total of three bead beating cycles
Place on magnetic rack, wait for the solution to become clear ~ 00:02:00 , and transfer the eluted DNA to a new well-labeled tube
Incubate at room temperature for 00:05:00
Immediately after the bead pellet becomes opaque, remove the tube from magnetic rack and resuspend in 20 µL of Low EDTA Tris Buffer . Ensure all beads are in solution.
Dry the beads briefly, ~ 00:02:00 . Tip: Remove residual EtOH with a p10 pipette
Discard the supernatant and wash the beads twice with freshly prepared 70 % (v/v) ethanol without disrupting the beads
Place on magnetic rack and wait for the solution to clear, ~ 00:02:00
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00
Add 250 µL (1.2X volume) AMPure XP beads and mix by pipetting up and down 10 times
Centrifuge at max speed 10000 rcf, Room temperature, 2 minutes and transfer the supernatant to a new well-labeled tube. Take care not to transfer beads or cell debris.
Repeat for a total of three bead beating cycles
Bead beat the lysate at 6.5m/s for 00:00:45 with 00:02:00 rest on ice between runs
Transfer the inactivated bacterial suspension to a new well-labeled Starsted screw cap tube containing ~ 250 µL of Mini-BeadBeater Zirconia-Silicate Beads, -0.1 mm
If required (i.e., to remove samples for processing outside a BSL-3), decontaminate the sample according to the standard operating procedures of your facility.
Vortex sample and rpm, °C, 15 minutes, max speed
Remove and discard the supernatant, and resuspend the pellet in 350 µL of Custom Triton Buffer
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:15:00
Add four volumes of 100mM dithiothreitol to the sputum sample and vortex for > 00:00:30

If it is not possible to do the above in your laboratory. Use BBL MycoPrepTM (BD) reagent to process the sample according to the manufacturer's instructions with the following modification: Resuspend the sediment in step 8 in the protocol in 350 µL of Custom Triton Buffer
Cut the bottom off a StarSted tube just below the inflection point. Cut the tip off a P1000 tip, cut a wedge near the end, and wedge the prepared StarSted tube in it.
Fill a sterile container (e.g., a Petri dish) with -0.1 mm Zirconia-Silicate Beads and use the prepared scoop to distribute them into 1.5ml StarSted tubes.
